1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/e2457da/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Ff4%2F14%2F0f4dd0fe447b87045b2c6f6659b1%2Fwestsubfolo-260402-06.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/8d43805/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Ff4%2F14%2F0f4dd0fe447b87045b2c6f6659b1%2Fwestsubfolo-260402-06.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Dr. Chidinma Osineme speaks to reporters Wednesday about conditions at West Suburban Medical Center.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Anthony Vazquez/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>The sudden closure, financial troubles and other ongoing issues have left the staff fed up with Prasad’s management, said Dr. Chidinma Osineme, a family medicine doctor at West Suburban and president of the medical staff.</p><p>“We are asking for new leadership to help us through this,” said Osineme, who talked to the Sun-Times in the hallway outside the room where the news conference was held; she said Prasad had barred her from the news conference. “We want to work collaboratively. We want a seat at the table.”</p><p>She said patients have reached out, saying they can't access their medical records and they're struggling to get referrals to other hospitals.</p><p>“Our community has come to this hospital every single day, regardless of what they're experiencing in their lives. Gunshots, yes. Addiction, yes. Cancer treatment, yes,” Osineme said. “These are lifesaving care that they need. And they come here and our doors are closed.”</p><p><i>Kristen Schorsch covers public health for WBEZ. Kaitlin Washburn is a Sun-Times staff reporter.</i></p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" data-embed> <iframe src="https://docs.google.com/viewer?embedded=true&url=https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/93/50/2efbd0c5424abd2f0f72d505a89f/west-suburban-hospital-inspection-260306.pdf"></iframe> </div> </div></div><p></p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" data-embed> <iframe src="https://docs.google.com/viewer?embedded=true&url=https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/70/88/6bb2d0ac4c0d93df0f1a65adda1d/west-suburban-hospital-inspection-251030.pdf"></iframe> </div> </div></div><p></p>

entries.title: 17 things to do in Chicago April 2-8
entries.summary: <h3>Theater</h3><ul class="rte2-style-ul" id="rte-6b575b90-2bdd-11f1-914c-4de3c64eaf0b"><li>A new take on Shakespeare’s raucous comedy, <b>“The Merry Wives of Windsor,”</b> reveals there is much more to the charming English hamlet than meets the eye as Mistress Page (Ora Jones) and Mistress Ford (Issy van Randwyck) find themselves the romantic targets of lovable rogue John Falstaff (Jason Simon). Phillip Breen directs. From April 2-May 3 at Chicago Shakespeare Theater, 800 W. Grand Ave. Tickets: $51+; $30 for age 30 and under. Visit <a class="Link" href="http://chicagoshakes.com" target="_blank" ><u>chicagoshakes.com</u></a>.</li><li>Subtext Studio Theatre presents<b> “Odessa,”</b> Dante Flores’ science fiction play about a young scientist at a lonely research facility in western Texas who investigates an extraterrestrial life form. Omar V. Fernandez directs the world premiere. From April 9-19 at Urban Theater Company, 2620 W. Division St. Tickets: $29.05. Visit <a class="Link" href="http://subtextstudiotc.org" target="_blank" ><u>subtextstudiotc.org</u></a>.</li><li><b>“Father of the Bride”</b> is Caroline Francke’s comedy, adapted from Edward Streeter’s novel, about a father who has a hard time letting go of his daughter when she announces her wedding plans. Michael Heitzman directs. From April 8-May 31 at Drury Lane Theatre, 100 Drury Lane, Oakbrook Terrace. Tickets: $85-$150. Visit <a class="Link" href="http://drurylanetheatre.com" target="_blank" ><u>drurylanetheatre.com</u></a>.</li><li>Eos Theatre presents Caroline Bird’s <b>“The Trojan Women,”</b> a modern-day version of Euripides’ anti-war play now set in a mother-and-baby unit of a prison. Rachel Sledd Iannantuoni directs. From April 2-18 at Bramble Arts Loft, 5545 N. Clark St. Tickets: $45. Visit <a class="Link" href="http://eostheatre.org" target="_blank" ><u>eostheatre.org</u></a>.</li></ul><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-520000" name="image-520000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/ff71f1f/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4576x2568+0+876/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe0%2Fbc%2F9fdd3cd74e8a8a62ab4094c6735c%2Fmrs-krishnans-party-courtesy-of-india-ink-theatre.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/0a2a23a/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4576x2568+0+876/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe0%2Fbc%2F9fdd3cd74e8a8a62ab4094c6735c%2Fmrs-krishnans-party-courtesy-of-india-ink-theatre.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/79a8bb0/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4576x2568+0+876/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe0%2Fbc%2F9fdd3cd74e8a8a62ab4094c6735c%2Fmrs-krishnans-party-courtesy-of-india-ink-theatre.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Mrs. Krishnan's Party - courtesy of India Ink Theatre.jpg" srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/79a8bb0/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4576x2568+0+876/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe0%2Fbc%2F9fdd3cd74e8a8a62ab4094c6735c%2Fmrs-krishnans-party-courtesy-of-india-ink-theatre.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/a250200/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4576x2568+0+876/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe0%2Fbc%2F9fdd3cd74e8a8a62ab4094c6735c%2Fmrs-krishnans-party-courtesy-of-india-ink-theatre.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/79a8bb0/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4576x2568+0+876/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fe0%2Fbc%2F9fdd3cd74e8a8a62ab4094c6735c%2Fmrs-krishnans-party-courtesy-of-india-ink-theatre.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>“Mrs. Krishnan’s Party” runs from April 7-26 at Chicago Shakespeare Theater, Upstairs Studio Theater, 800 E. Grand Ave.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>India Ink Theatre</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p></p><ul class="rte2-style-ul" id="rte-0d9822f0-2be7-11f1-9b8f-81798c36fc02"><li>New Zealand’s Indian Ink Theatre presents <b>“Mrs. Krishnan’s Party,”</b> an immersive work set in the back room of a corner shop where Mrs. Krishnan (Kalyani Nagarajan) is throwing a party like no other. From April 7-26 at Chicago Shakespeare Theater, Upstairs Studio Theater, 800 E. Grand Ave. Tickets: $49+; $30 for age 30 and under. Visit <a class="Link" href="http://chicagoshakes.com" target="_blank" ><u>chicagoshakes.com</u></a>.</li><li>Lee Osorio’s new play, <b>“A Third Way,”</b> reimagines what love, marriage and family can look like in the 21st century. Tours run through late fall and depart from the Ogden Slip located at River East Plaza, 465 N. McClurg Court. For more information on this and other specialty tours, visit <a class="Link" href="http://chicagoline.com" target="_blank" ><u>chicagoline.com</u></a>.</li></ul>
entries.author: Mary Houlihan

entries.title: Mayor Johnson looks to oust CHA board chair after agency votes in new CEO
entries.summary: <p>Mayor Brandon Johnson and Chicago Housing Authority Board Chair Matthew Brewer’s fight over the future of the city's housing agency escalated Wednesday when Johnson said he removed Brewer from his board chair role, while Brewer said he isn’t going anywhere.</p><p>The CHA board recently voted 7-2 to approve the appointment of <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/real-estate/2026/03/17/chicago-housing-authority-appoints-keith-pettigrew-as-new-ceo" >Keith Pettigrew</a> as the agency’s new CEO for a four-year term. Pettigrew is currently the executive director of the District of Columbia Housing Authority. The CHA has been without a CEO for 16 months.</p><p>The vote marked a win for the board over Johnson, who pushed for the appointment of mayoral ally, retired <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/city-hall/2025/08/08/mayor-brandon-johnson-walter-burnett-chicago-housing-authority-ceo-appointment-conflict-hud" >Ald. Walter Burnett</a>. </p><p>Johnson said Wednesday that he also removed Brewer from his role as the CHA’s interim operating chairman, but it's unclear if the mayor followed the proper procedures to do so. Brewer argues the mayor hasn’t.</p><p>The CHA board’s bylaws state the board chair is designated by the mayor and a “filing of the Certificate of Appointment with the Recorder of Deeds of Cook County,” whose responsibilities fall under the Cook County clerk’s office.</p><p>As of Wednesday afternoon, the clerk’s records show no documents related to Johnson’s appointment of CHA Board Commissioner Jawanza Malone as the new chair.</p><p>Johnson also can’t boot Brewer as operating chairman since that decision is controlled by the board, Brewer said.</p><p>“They are wrong on the facts, and they are wrong on the law,” Brewer told the Sun-Times. “If this is just an attempt to bully and scare us to back down, I don’t think it is going to happen.”</p><p>Johnson insisted he was on firm ground acting against Brewer, arguing in part that Pettigrew's appointment wasn't legal because proper notice of the resolution to appoint a new CEO at the March 17 meeting was not made. Brewer's "stewardship of this process disenfranchised both fellow commissioners and the communities the CHA serves," Johnson said. "CHA residents deserve leadership decisions that are transparent, lawful, and grounded in their lived experiences, not a process that prioritizes expediency over accountability."</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-010000" name="image-010000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/349b592/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6238x3501+0+330/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F2f%2Fba%2Fcc690135437f965e3654e1374dcc%2Fcouncil-260122-42.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/f2ee103/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6238x3501+0+330/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F2f%2Fba%2Fcc690135437f965e3654e1374dcc%2Fcouncil-260122-42.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/39786a9/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6238x3501+0+330/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F2f%2Fba%2Fcc690135437f965e3654e1374dcc%2Fcouncil-260122-42.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="A man in a suit speaks into two microphones at a lectern emblazoned with the city of Chicago seal. A Chicago flag is behind him. " srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/39786a9/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6238x3501+0+330/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F2f%2Fba%2Fcc690135437f965e3654e1374dcc%2Fcouncil-260122-42.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/15783a8/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6238x3501+0+330/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F2f%2Fba%2Fcc690135437f965e3654e1374dcc%2Fcouncil-260122-42.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/39786a9/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6238x3501+0+330/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F2f%2Fba%2Fcc690135437f965e3654e1374dcc%2Fcouncil-260122-42.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Mayor Brandon Johnson announced Wednesday he is ousting Matthew Brewer from his chairmanship roles at the Chicago Housing Authority. </p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Ashlee Rezin/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Chicago's mayor has historically chosen the leader of the CHA, with the board vote typically serving as a rubber stamp of the mayor’s candidate of choice.</p><p>Commissioners Jawanza Malone and <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/2026/03/13/chicago-housing-authority-board-commissioner-debra-parker-section-8-voucher" >Debra Parker</a> voted no last month on Pettigrew's appointment, while Commissioner Mildred Harris abstained. Brewer has asked the mayor to remove Parker from the board, after the agency’s independent inspector general investigated her, and a hearing officer for the CHA last month determined that she committed what the officer described as <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/the-watchdogs/2026/03/27/cha-commissioner-debra-parker-drops-lawsuit-against-the-housing-authority-ending-fight-to-keep-voucher" >fraud</a>.</p><p>The CHA board members who voted to appoint Pettigrew reaffirmed their commitment on Wednesday in a public statement: “The public vote held during the Board meeting on Tuesday, March 17, 2026, regarding the appointment of Keith Pettigrew as chief executive officer, was not a unilateral action by Operating Chairman Brewer; rather, it reflected a duly recognized and transparent vote of the Board. Board members were informed of Mr. Pettigrew’s selection, and the affirmative public votes of seven board members confirmed the appointment.”</p><p></p><p></p><p><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/the-watchdogs/2025/11/15/cha-residents-frustrated-housing-agency-permanent-ceo" ></a></p><p></p><p>The board has been unable to consider Burnett, whom many CHA residents opposed, as a candidate until the agency receives conflict waivers approved by the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development. The waiver request were waiting on a final determination from staff at HUD’s headquarters in Washington, D.C.</p><p>Burnett’s apparent conflicts of interest, according to the CHA and HUD, were with his 30-year record as alderman and longtime ownership of properties rented to housing voucher holders. Since 2007, Burnett and his wife have collected <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/real-estate/2025/09/12/alderman-walter-burnett-wife-rent-payments-cha-housing-voucher-landlords-hud" >more than $260,000</a> as CHA voucher landlords.</p><p>Johnson’s break with the CHA board is part of his ongoing power struggle with sister agencies that used to be an extension of the mayor’s power.</p><p>Johnson initially retained Pedro Martinez, the Chicago Public Schools CEO appointed by former Mayor Lori Lightfoot, then fired him after he refused to authorize a short-term, high-interest loan to reimburse the city for a $175 million pension payment for nonteaching school employees.</p><p>The Chicago Board of Education that included Johnson’s own appointees sided with Martinez and resigned en masse.</p><p>The CTA board also defied Johnson. When embattled CTA President Dorval Carter Jr. resigned, the mayor tried to install Chief Operating Officer John Roberson as Carter’s permanent replacement, but the board refused. Roberson ended up leaving City Hall for a job as executive vice president for the Obama Presidential Center.</p><p>The mayor also offered the CTA job to state Rep. Kam Buckner, but the former mayoral challenger who specializes in mass transit issues turned it down. As a result, Nora Leerhsen has served as interim CTA president for nearly 18 months.</p><p>If Johnson doesn’t name a permanent CTA president by the June 1 deadline established as part of the Illinois General Assembly’s mass transit bailout, he will lose the power to make that choice. It’ll be made for him by the newly created Northern Illinois Transportation Authority that replaces the RTA board.</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/real-estate/2026/03/17/chicago-housing-authority-appoints-keith-pettigrew-as-new-ceo" >Chicago Housing Authority appoints Keith Pettigrew as new CEO</a> </li> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/the-watchdogs/2026/03/27/cha-commissioner-debra-parker-drops-lawsuit-against-the-housing-authority-ending-fight-to-keep-voucher" >CHA Commissioner Debra Parker ends fight to keep housing voucher, may resign from board</a> </li> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/city-hall/2025/08/08/mayor-brandon-johnson-walter-burnett-chicago-housing-authority-ceo-appointment-conflict-hud" >Retired Ald. Walter Burnett’s jump to top CHA post stalled</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p></p>
entries.author: Lizzie Kane

entries.title: DePaul student charged with spitting on ICE agent at O'Hare, but lawyer says felony case is overblown
entries.summary: <p>A DePaul University student was charged with spitting on an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent working at O’Hare Airport amid a partial government shutdown prompted by calls to reform ICE.</p><p>Brett Heier, 20, "intentionally and forcefully" spat on the agent Sunday evening while leaving Terminal 2 at O’Hare, according to a Chicago police arrest report. Heier faces a felony charge of aggravated battery to a peace officer.</p><p>But his lawyer, Barry Sheppard, argued that his client is only accused of spitting on the agent's shoe, and it may have been unintentional. The arrest report notes that Heier was taking medication for an abscessed tooth.</p><p>"In the past, [ICE] seems to have made smaller incidents larger than life," Sheppard said in an interview, "and I feel this one was also blown out of proportion."</p><p>A spokesperson for ICE said its employees “are grappling with a 1,300% increase in assaults, a 3,200% increase in vehicle attacks and a staggering 8,000% uptick in death threats as they put their lives on the line to arrest murderers, rapists, pedophiles, and gang members.”</p><p>The ICE agent who was allegedly hit by the spit identified Heier’s photo in a lineup, along with a second agency official. Chicago cops arrested Heier Tuesday as he left his Lincoln Park apartment, and he was ordered released from custody during his first court hearing Wednesday. </p><p>The alleged spitting incident at O’Hare came a week after President Donald Trump announced he was <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/news/2026/03/23/ice-agents-ohare-airport-partial-government-shutdown" target="_blank" >sending ICE agents to airports</a> across the country to assist with security amid the fight over DHS funding.</p><p>The resulting government shutdown has led to long lines at airports and staffing issues among TSA agents, who went unpaid from mid-February until Trump signed an <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/travel/2026/03/26/tsa-travel-airports-dhs-ice-ohare-federal-budget-congress-impasse-government-shutdown" target="_blank" >executive order giving them back pay</a> last week.</p><p>Employees of other DHS agencies, including ICE and Customs and Border Protection, have continued to earn paychecks because they’re being funded by Trump’s domestic policy bill passed over the summer.</p><p>The shutdown was prompted by Democrats’ unanswered calls for reforms after two U.S. citizens were fatally shot during an aggressive deportation campaign in Minnesota earlier this year.</p><p>Meantime, Cook County State’s Attorney Eileen O’Neill Burke has faced mounting calls to investigate and prosecute immigration agents who carried out a similar operation in the Chicago area. She has said she’s legally handcuffed when it comes to targeting the feds, even as legal efforts have been launched to <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/immigration/2026/03/24/chicago-cook-county-trump-ice-border-patrol-midway-blitz-special-prosecutor-oneill-burke-loevy" target="_blank" >install a special prosecutor</a> in her office to do just that.</p><p>A spokesperson for the state's attorney declined to comment on Heier’s case and the decision to charge him with a felony.</p><p>Heier’s attorney, Sheppard, said his client’s alleged actions fall under the "broad gamut of freedom of expression."</p><p>"He's a very good young man who does well in college, never had any type of arrest for anything before," Sheppard said.</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/news/2026/03/23/ice-agents-ohare-airport-partial-government-shutdown" target="_blank" >ICE agents deployed at O’Hare as partial government shutdown continues to impact travelers</a> </li> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/immigration/2026/03/27/activists-protest-ice-agents-at-ohare-airport" target="_blank" >Activists protest ICE agents at O’Hare Airport</a> </li> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/immigration/2026/03/24/chicago-cook-county-trump-ice-border-patrol-midway-blitz-special-prosecutor-oneill-burke-loevy" target="_blank" >O’Neill Burke rejects bid for special prosecutor to target ICE, says it threatens to ‘thwart’ justice</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p></p>
entries.author: Frank Main
